---
title: "A Field Guide to Statistics and Biostatistics Journals"
subtitle: "For students in semiparametric statistics, causal inference, and targeted learning"
author: "Compiled for UC Berkeley Biostatistics & the Center for Targeted Machine Learning and Causal Inference"
date: today
---

# Purpose of this guide

This document is a working map of the journal landscape for students and early-career researchers in biostatistics, causal inference, and semiparametric theory — particularly those affiliated with the UC Berkeley Division of Biostatistics and the Center for Targeted Machine Learning and Causal Inference (CTML). It is meant to help you:

- Decide where to submit a given paper
- Understand what each venue's editorial culture and audience actually rewards
- Develop taste for which venues are prestigious within our subfield (which does not always track bibliometrics)
- Think strategically about the tenure clock, the job market, and the arc of an early career

Tier lists are inherently opinionated. Treat the rankings as a starting point for conversation with your advisor, not as fact. Fit matters more than tier; a well-placed paper at a specialty journal that reaches your actual audience is worth more than a grudgingly accepted paper at a generalist one.

---

# The tier list at a glance

## Main ranking (semiparametrics and causal inference lens)

```{=html}
<div class="tier-row">
  <a class="tier-label tier-s-label" href="tiers.html#s-tier">S</a>
  <div class="tier-body">
    <div class="tier-caption">Where the canon gets written</div>
    <div class="tier-journals">
      <a class="journal-pill tier-s-pill" href="tiers.html#annals-of-statistics">Annals of Statistics</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-s-pill" href="tiers.html#jasa-tm">JASA (T&amp;M)</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-s-pill" href="tiers.html#jrss-b">JRSS-B</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-s-pill" href="tiers.html#biometrika">Biometrika</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="tier-row">
  <a class="tier-label tier-a-label" href="tiers.html#a-tier">A</a>
  <div class="tier-body">
    <div class="tier-caption">Core causal/semiparametric homes</div>
    <div class="tier-journals">
      <a class="journal-pill tier-a-pill" href="tiers.html#biometrics">Biometrics</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-a-pill" href="tiers.html#biostatistics">Biostatistics</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-a-pill" href="tiers.html#jasa-acs">JASA (A&amp;CS)</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-a-pill" href="tiers.html#epidemiology">Epidemiology</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-a-pill" href="tiers.html#j-econometrics">J. Econometrics</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-a-pill" href="tiers.html#jmlr">JMLR</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-a-pill" href="tiers.html#econometrica">Econometrica</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-a-pill" href="tiers.html#ijb">Int. J. Biostatistics</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-a-pill" href="tiers.html#j-causal-inference">J. Causal Inference</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="tier-row">
  <a class="tier-label tier-b-label" href="tiers.html#b-tier">B</a>
  <div class="tier-body">
    <div class="tier-caption">Strong &amp; relevant, often audience-specific</div>
    <div class="tier-journals">
      <a class="journal-pill tier-b-pill" href="tiers.html#aoas">AoAS</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-b-pill" href="tiers.html#aje">Am. J. Epidemiology</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-b-pill" href="tiers.html#statistical-science">Statistical Science</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-b-pill" href="tiers.html#stats-in-medicine">Statistics in Medicine</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-b-pill" href="tiers.html#bernoulli">Bernoulli</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-b-pill" href="tiers.html#jrss-a">JRSS-A</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-b-pill" href="tiers.html#restat">Rev. Econ. &amp; Stat.</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-b-pill" href="tiers.html#observational-studies">Observational Studies</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-b-pill" href="tiers.html#ejs">Electronic J. Stat.</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-b-pill" href="tiers.html#pharm-stats">Pharmaceutical Statistics</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="tier-row">
  <a class="tier-label tier-c-label" href="tiers.html#c-tier">C</a>
  <div class="tier-body">
    <div class="tier-caption">Respectable niche / methods-applied outlets</div>
    <div class="tier-journals">
      <a class="journal-pill tier-c-pill" href="tiers.html#smmr">Stat. Methods Med. Res.</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-c-pill" href="tiers.html#j-clin-epi">J. Clinical Epidemiology</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-c-pill" href="tiers.html#hsr">Health Services Research</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-c-pill" href="tiers.html#j-health-econ">J. Health Economics</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-c-pill" href="tiers.html#lifetime-data-analysis">Lifetime Data Analysis</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-c-pill" href="tiers.html#scand-j-stat">Scand. J. Stat.</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-c-pill" href="tiers.html#econometric-theory">Econometric Theory</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-c-pill" href="tiers.html#csda">CSDA</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-c-pill" href="tiers.html#jrss-c">JRSS-C</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-c-pill" href="tiers.html#biometrical-journal">Biometrical Journal</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## ML conferences (parallel track)

```{=html}
<div class="tier-row">
  <a class="tier-label tier-conf-label" href="ml-conferences.html">ML</a>
  <div class="tier-body">
    <div class="tier-caption">Peer-reviewed venues where much causal-ML work now lives</div>
    <div class="tier-journals">
      <a class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill" href="ml-conferences.html">NeurIPS</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill" href="ml-conferences.html">ICML</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill" href="ml-conferences.html">AISTATS</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill" href="ml-conferences.html">ICLR</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill" href="ml-conferences.html">UAI</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill" href="ml-conferences.html">COLT</a>
      <a class="journal-pill tier-conf-pill" href="ml-conferences.html">CLeaR</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

---

## How to read this guide

- [**What makes a journal prestigious**](prestige.qmd) — the dimensions that actually matter when we say one journal is "better" than another
- [**The tier list**](tiers.qmd) — detailed notes on every venue, grouped by tier, with 1–3 representative papers each:
  - [S tier](tiers.qmd#s-tier) — the field-defining flagships
  - [A tier](tiers.qmd#a-tier) — core homes for semiparametric and causal work
  - [B tier](tiers.qmd#b-tier) — strong and relevant, often audience-specific
  - [C tier](tiers.qmd#c-tier) — respectable niche and methods-applied outlets
- [**ML conferences**](ml-conferences.qmd) — the parallel CS-culture track
- [**Strategy & practice**](strategy.qmd) — submission strategy, R&R, the tenure clock, mentorship
